contradiction_text: The article’s security prevalence claim depends on a noisy identification pipeline in which many pull requests that looked security-related at the keyword stage did not survive manual review, and some security-relevant pull requests may have been missed entirely because non-keyword cases were never reviewed.
related_claim_texts: In the curated AIDev dataset, 1,293 of 33,596 agent-authored pull requests were classified as security-related, or 3.85%.
disclosure: The 3.85% prevalence estimate should remain visible as a pipeline-bounded result, not a complete measure of all security-relevant agent-authored pull requests.

contradiction_text: The article frames agent-authored security work as leaning toward supportive hardening rather than narrow vulnerability fixing, but the evidence also shows a substantial vulnerability-mitigation presence, so the pattern is mixed rather than exclusive.
related_claim_texts: Agent-authored security PRs in this study more often reflected supportive security hardening work such as refactoring, testing, and documentation than only narrow vulnerability-fix activity.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that supportive hardening is the dominant described pattern, but vulnerability mitigation remains materially present and should not be written out of the picture.

contradiction_text: The article interprets lower merge rates and much longer review latency as evidence of more cautious review for security-related pull requests, but the source also shows strong variation across tools and languages, so the cautious-review pattern is real at the aggregate level without being uniform across contexts.
related_claim_texts: Security-related agent-authored PRs were reviewed more cautiously than non-security PRs, with a lower merge rate and much longer median review latency.
disclosure: The aggregate review-friction result should stay visible, but it should be disclosed that merge outcomes vary substantially by tool and language and do not support a flat universal account.

contradiction_text: One agent-level distribution detail is flagged in the evidence as numerically inconsistent with the curated subset totals, creating unresolved tension around how confidently per-agent breakdowns should be read even though the article’s higher-level claims do not depend on that detail.
related_claim_texts: Security-related agent-authored PRs were reviewed more cautiously than non-security PRs, with a lower merge rate and much longer median review latency.
disclosure: The article should keep visible that at least one agent-level sentence in the source appears internally inconsistent, so per-agent distribution details warrant caution even if the broader dataset findings remain usable.
